title: halesia carolina subsp. monticola
intro: Halesia carolina subsp. monticola, also known as the mountain silverbell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Pentaphylacaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Halesia carolina subsp. monticola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
